global_context.scene_description: A dramatic close-up photograph of a modern white digital wall thermostat mounted on a beige interior wall. The thermostat display is glowing bright red/orange and showing a temperature reading of 115 degrees Fahrenheit. The plastic housing of the thermostat is visibly warped and melting — the edges are drooping, bubbling, and deforming as if exposed to extreme heat. The wall paint around the thermostat is cracking, peeling, and blistering from heat damage in a radial pattern. There is a visible heat shimmer or heat distortion effect in the air around the thermostat. The scene is lit with intense warm orange-red lighting from the right side suggesting brutal sunlight pouring through a window, while the left side falls into darker shadow. A small smartphone is propped against the wall nearby showing a notification on screen reading 'HVAC Install — EARLIEST AVAILABLE: AUGUST 2'. The overall mood is oppressive, uncomfortable, and urgent — this is what waiting too long looks like.
global_context.weather_atmosphere: Oppressive heat, heat shimmer visible in air

lighting.source: Harsh sunlight from window on right side
lighting.direction: Strong side-light from right, creating dramatic shadows on left
lighting.quality: Hard, harsh, unforgiving — like Arizona summer sun

composition.camera_angle: Straight-on, eye level with thermostat, slight macro feel
composition.framing: Close-up to medium, thermostat fills center 40% of frame
composition.depth_of_field: Shallow — thermostat tack sharp, wall and edges softly out of focus
composition.focal_point: The melting thermostat display showing 115 degrees

visual_attributes.state: Melting, warped, distorted from extreme heat

Plastic drooping downward at bottom edge
Small bubbles forming on plastic surface
Edge of housing separating from wall

Subtle heat shimmer distortion in the air

visual_attributes.state: Lit screen showing a scheduling notification

Notification has a red alert icon

semantic_relationships: The thermostat is the central focus, showing the extreme temperature
The melting plastic communicates the severity of the heat visually
The damaged wall radiates outward from the thermostat suggesting prolonged heat exposure
The phone notification in the corner delivers the 'too late' message — you waited and now you can't get an install
Warm orange lighting reinforces the oppressive heat atmosphere
The overall scene makes the viewer feel the discomfort of waiting too long
